reviews.review_title: Not a 5* because miss of opportunity
reviews.review_description: I don't know if it's a hardware issue or something else, but this game could be a Black and white like game, a bit bigger and a bit more action, . I enjoyed the game don't get me wrong, but the lack of content and not being a bit bigger of a game gets me confused.. So much potential and a category of game that people like and enjoy so much, look at age of empires 2, a lot of people still play that game, so old and still so much bigger than this. This is a problem in vr, it feels every game is a demo, nothing else.

I got this game as part of the subscription service and I was shocked at how cool it was. There are various elements of city builder,  god game, survival, and tower defense. 

I booted it up to try out ended up playing for hours. I continued to play every chance I got throughout the week until I beat it.  

The basic premise is you go to an island, and you have to secure the basics of food and water so your people survive. There are a variety of resources and more advanced buildings as tone goes on and the tower defense aspect gradually ramps up as well. It is very well balanced, and is never frustrating. 

Excellent all around. I highly recommend this one.

So i truly enjoyed playing this game. It kept me busy for a few months. I didnt want to see it end, but I felt like it gave a very fair bang for the buck. I would gladly play again if new levels were created. Thank you to the developers for creating an enjoyable experience.

reviews.review_title: Great game let down on technical grounds
reviews.review_description:
Why are you here? Did you want gods but VR? Close. Close enough. No, its not the same. Its it's own thing. Its a tale of a king's employee rescuing the kingdom. tbh its not story that makes it and merely leads the consequences. But then, did gods really give you some sort of Epiphone with the story?Its the different islands looking to need a certain set of solutions, just like gods. Yes, you get this. You move around using your hands to drag the island around. You get no pets :( There is no analogue for this either but it wasnt Gods specific. Come on! Its not like they could just copy Gods for heavens sake.

 All being said, its fantastic with low key, folky music that works to the environment. To the gods fans, its about 60% gods in feel. Its about 30% Gods in scale. As a package, the team nailed their brief. 

Technical: It struggles like many god sims with framerate. If you get sick from motion, this wont be a besty. Worst technical is a memory leak that kills the quest 2 after long play. I cant say the quest 3 or 3s wont suffer the same but this feels like a quest 3/3S will feel so much better. (I dont have a Q3 to test this on but i know its 2x performance and 1.5 resolution)
Quest 1 - Why are you even here?
Quest2 - Expect liveable compromises (use sidequest to overclock or under-res )
Quest 3/3S  - This game was meant for you without being official.

I enjoyed playing this game. I've completed all the levels and now I'm sad trying to find a similar game like this one. Maybe they could make a new game and colonize planets and fight aliens instead of pirates!!!

I played each level many times and also sandbox in different ways. I wish they can develop more environments for sandbox. I played it 2 or 3 hours everyday for the past 2 months. I think I have played the game in every possible way.
I’ve been playing this game for three days. There are absolutely no speed-up mechanics in the game, so I’m forced to just wait—slowly! Aside from draining my battery, I don't find it fun at all.

I remember, probably 20 years ago, I played Townsmen on my cousin's flip phone for the first time, and now I'm just amazed to the core by how cool the strategy game from my childhood has been implemented in a VR world. It's incredible!

First off I want to say for how amazing this game is it’s crazy to see any hate. I did not play this when it first came out so maybe there were problems I didn’t encounter but for any negative review that actually didn’t have problems with bugs or glitching has to be lying to a degree. It has a bit of a slow start which is still enjoyable but once u get towards the end it’s so addicting. I really hope they make another one because this game blew my mind.

reviews.review_title: Amazing story! Really hope for a sequel.
reviews.review_description:
This game is truly captivating! Unlike many other VR strategies, it has a great story campaign. Following the journey of the King, his daughter, the brave Knight, and the scheming Uncle was a lot of fun. The characters are charming, and the fairytale atmosphere keeps you hooked for hours.
The Sandbox mode is also well-done and polished, but like any sandbox, it eventually gets a bit repetitive. I really crave more of that story-driven content.
I sincerely hope the developers release a sequel or a new campaign DLC. Townsmen VR is one of the best diorama-style games I've played. Highly recommended!

Multiplayer would be cool. Either going against each other or working together to make a good civilization

Also adding more building like stables to tame horses and make transportation of goods faster. 

Also making walls to protect yourself from pirates. Or making more siege weaponry like defensive trebuchets or ballistas.

Overall this game is great, but multiplayer would be kind of cool I think
I really enjoyed this game. It’s the only one I’ve played really on the VR. I enjoy playing again as well. Needs more levels!!! Great game love it
Townsmen has been the first game that was both comfortable to play in VR and captivating in terms of gameplay.  5/5 RTS for those who wish to play solo. Bravo!

The game is entertaining and addictive. It's not perfect; there are things that could be improved, but the idea and approach work perfectly. I think the main problem is that it's a little slow. Maybe that could be solved with something to speed up the time or make it go faster, although once you get into the story and see all the possibilities, it's incredible. I hope they keep updating it and improving it. 

Another thing, I have a problem. I would like to complete your game 100%. I like it and I want to do it, but there is a problem with one of the achievements, Acupuncture. I've tried hundreds of times, but it doesn't work. There's no way to stop the arrow once it's shot. It just goes through my hand. I don't know if I'm doing something wrong or if it's a bug, but if it is, I hope you can fix it =3.

Its and amazing game with some great mechanics but there's a couple things that othet people may agree on:the resource collecting is slow,the building is slow,the npcs are extremely slow and the npc's get hungry and thirsty every 5 second so they can't do much so please fix this also on the third to last island when the pirated show up they quite literally take all of your freaking resources fix you fricking game or I'm never playing again!

I’m really enjoying it.  It takes slightly too long for resources to be available and for people to walk around the island getting supplies to build new things.  You can save your game and it’s fun to play with the clouds and cat and interact.  I’m a bit hooked, but I’m not sure how much I will play it again when I’m done.  I’ll let you know.
This game is just amazing. I never enjoyed game so much.. love the story and the strategy behind. Amazing wish it will continue forever

Such an amazing game and it is very addicting I played several hours without realizing it each time I played there's always something to do so you're interested in a game that you can dive in and lose track of time have fun playing it

The game is amazing. I enjoyed playing it all the way through. It’s got a pretty decent storyline to it, but is there any more any updates coming out anytime soon? multiplayer maybe ? But all in all pretty great game. ￼
It really needs multiplayer me and my friend love playing it but we would really enjoy it if it has multiplayer but overall great game I’ve already pulled all nighter playing this game
Highly recommend this game, the only thing I wish is an update with a new story as i have finished the game twice in hard mode. Please add a new story!!

reviews.review_title: This game is good, but kind of free not
reviews.review_description: To hard to move ￼

I really enjoyed this game for the gameplay. The voice acting, character writing, and story wasn't great but i guess it's passable for adolescents which the game seems targeted for, which is fine and easy to look past. My biggest gripes are the performance and graphics quality. Quality when zoomed out isn't good. There's also some pop in, build menu and characters sometimes run at substantially lower frame rate, noticable frame dips, and some lag outs on tracking. If only it was better optimized the game would be a great experience. It seems like the devs tried to balance the performance and graphics quality but I believe it should run better on both fronts on my 3s.
Enjoyed this game to the fullest. Hope there are DLCs coming out soon

I absolutely love this game.  It starts slowly so gives 
 you a chance to get the hang of it.  Once you get in to it you find yourself addicted to the game.  I look forward to seeing what the creators come up with next. Definitely recommend people give this game a proper go
Better than Deisim by a long shot. Just the right amount of challenging to keep you hooked. Only complaint is that I finished it too soon!

Got this on Horizon+ this month. On level 11 and have been enjoying it so far.  Two minor issues; I can't figure out how to heal (an earlier level mentioned clerics + herb garden but they don't seem to be healing my injured troops), and I wish there was a way to set priorities.  For instance, I finally get a few honeycombs, and my units decide to eat that rather than the plentiful meat or fish or stew that's available, or lumberjacks that keep turning logs into planks, leaving me with no logs to build with, or a cook that keeps getting more fish when water is what's really needed.

Outside of those minor issues (that can be worked around), it's been a pleasant way to spent some free time.  :-)
Downloaded this game just for something to do, become instantly addicted, was fun to play hopefully there will be a second one

It is fun for like 2 seconds then it is super boring. The islands are cool. The main thing about this game is that it takes for ever to finish an island and the game does not save, if you are busy with many things then you do NOT want this game and if you don’t do much like literally nothing then you might like it. But it does get very boring.

developer_response.body: Hi, about the game not saving. You should be able to save the game at any time yourself and it also auto-saves for you when you get to a new level or haven't saved in the last 5 minutes.

reviews.review_description: I want to love this game, and I think I would, but I struggled in the tutorial with getting information from the info button above chests.  I closed it and went back in to see if I missed an instruction or something in the tutorial.  As soon as I went back in, the info boxes worked.  Played for another few minutes and need to check an info button on something on the actual gameplay, and again, the info icons did not work.  I have not changed any of the default settings for the controls.  I press down the thumb stick, and nothing happens.  Closed out and have not gone back in.

I love the game and everything in it but the townsmen glitch ALOT they will stand around and do nothing even when you try to force them to do said task example being cooks not doing anything when there is 0 water buckets so you force them to the well only for them to walk away and idle. Lowkey a buzz kill I hope the devs fix it soon.
Playing this game gives me nostalgia vibes of early games like Black and White or Populus! Definatly worth checking it out, with the appropriate time on your VR hands.
This is a great resource management town builder with a rts vibe.  Must own!!
I like building up the islands. I wish it had a passthrough mode. I like to play for hours while binge watching shows.

I had an incredibly enjoyable experience with this game! It's stunning, entertaining, and addictive. Unlike the mobile version, playing in VR feels almost magical. You can interact with the characters and animals by getting close and picking them up. I didn't expect to complete all the islands in campaign mode before losing interest, but I felt a bit sad when I reached the end. Now, I plan to explore all the islands in sandbox mode. I highly recommend this game for all ages; it's a great way for kids to develop various skills, and it’s also lots of fun for adults.

Pathing and sub optimal behaviors of the citizens, especially for wood gathering. Pace is SLOW. Actions on an object are queued by the person starting to come TO the object, not arriving at it. Therefore, 5 people may refuse to drink water if one person is walking from the opposite side of the island to get water. This is painfully lazy programming and causes you to have to sit and just ... wait. Which is bad enough in normal games, but especially painful in VR.

Unlike many VR games, this is one that can take up hours of your time. It’s essentially Age of Empires in VR. There’s not much replay-ability after beating the game. If there was a multiplayer option I’d still play this game, as there are no good multiplayer RTS games in VR yet.
Fingers crossed for a sequel. Replayabilty would be better if they had different difficulty settings!
it really is. Graphics are simple. But game is fun and easy to get in to. It reminds me of simplyfied version of Black and White series.

Hopefully this is the start of great city building in VR ,this is a great game to showcase the possibilities and I have spent hours in this.
The detail is great and it is addictive to want to keep playing, I bought this on sale and it was well worth the price.

I really lose my sense of time when playing city builders and strategy games and this is one of them that I have been playing the most. A recommend playing this game when I hope that there are more things to add in this game and other similar games to come.

i have meta horizon + so i got this game for free but for anyone thinking of actually buying the game i wouldn't. when you first load in and hover over the start button it instantly kicks you of the game. i've tried uninstalling the game and restarting my oculus but it still isn't fixing. i'll update my review when this bug is fixed and i can actually play the game.

developer_response.body: Hi and sorry for this crash. Did you try to restart the game after the crash without reinstalling it? There was a crash earlier during development that we thought to be fixed that only happened during a first start of the game. When starting it a second time, the crash would not appear anymore. If it is the same issue, reinstalling the game would have reset this behaviour and make it crash again.

reviews.review_description: Very addictive, well done.
Loooooveee it
I gave this game an honest 5 minutes, thats all I could handle.  The basic movements and information system is not traditional and I know I won't be able to get used to it.

Townsmen VR has been my favourite VR game so far, offering a charming and immersive city-building experience that feels amazing in virtual reality. If you enjoy games like The Sims or Age of Empires, this is absolutely for you, managing villagers, constructing buildings, and shaping your settlement with your own hands makes the whole experience both relaxing and addictive. It's a perfect blend of strategy and VR interaction, and easily one of the most enjoyable world-building games on the headset.
W game
Great idea, game play, BUT movement, oh holy hell....Motion Sick
The game does not make sense and is glitchy and boring waisted money

Loving this game at the moment, only an hr in but brings back old memories of playing the settlers when i was a kid. Obviously alot better quality than back then and love been able to zoom in and out for closer inspection
Thought this would be like world box were you already have sandbox unlocked and you can kill them and drown them

Intriguing. Profound.

Comparison is the thief of joy.

Townsmen VR is kind of a down-beat low-grade parody of Smurfs Magical Meadows somehow shoehorned into VR. Whereas SMM is baroque, TVR is rather Gothic.
VR is still in its early stages and as yet, its reach exceeds its grasp.
However Townsmen VR is very playable and repayable. Space is extremely limited and buildings must be strategically arranged down to the last millimetre, it seems. Selection is limited, slim pickins. Toy Trains VR is a World builder also with a limited selection of buildings, plants, animals, etc.

Townsmen VR is the best World builder VR app that I've seen so far. The graphics are very beautiful and painterly. Other VR World builders have Nintendo 64 level graphics.

I played this one before on PCVR, now replaying the campaign on Quest 3. It is truly a lovely, charming game. The gameplay works really well. Watching your workers build up the island never gets old. Helping them complete their chores is very satisfying. Hope the devs are working on a worthy sequel.

reviews.review_title: Takes me back to playing settlers 2
reviews.review_description: VR game that reminds me of playing the settlers 2 on my dad's PC. The mechanics can be a little clunky at times but that's a small point for a great game.

When I open this game i only exit when the charge is empty
Best game ever
I bought this because it was on sale for $10.99, and I’m glad I didn’t spend the full almost $30. It’s a cute game but it’s not worth 30 bucks, it’s about worth the $10. ￼
I am told to activate info and press a button on my chest. There is no button on my chest. I've pressed every button. This game does not work properly. Can't even play the tutorial

developer_response.body: Hi, to show the info icons above the chests, press down on one of the thumb sticks. Then you can point at them with a ray coming from your index finger and click them with the A button. Depending on which configuration you are using, the left (by default) or right thumbstick might also be setup to rotate and zoom the camera. In that case use the thumbstick on the other controller.

reviews.review_description: Build towns on many islands
Ive been enjoying the game. I like how you can pick up the people and move them around or throw them around. You can zoom in real close and be a part of the town or watch from above.
I fell in love with this game. It’s the first VR I  played all the way through. The only thing I hope the devs add more stories or DLC as I would certainly pay more for some new adventures!
I killed the castle with a catapult

reviews.review_title: Could use without the level system
reviews.review_description: As I played the first level I thought this is exactly the game I've been looking for. Then I realized you only finish the levels to completely restart again. I wanted a civ builder and its almost the same.
Love this game!! MORE PLEASE :)

I'm loving this game. It's quirky, fun and engrossing. There a good range of buildings to research and build. Trading, pirates, animals and lots of townsmen to advocate jobs to.

And I had a couple of things I couldn't figure out so contacted the dev team. They came back to me with answers, so great support too.
It's really nice after getting used to control:)

I don't think this game is boring at all, it's slow and easy going if you just want to sit and relax
There isn't much in the way of features but as a concept it's brilliant 
If you could pick up and move buildings and interact with more stuff it would be better but it's still a good game

Not perfect but such a fun game- great graphics and it's cool to be able to grab birds, animals, clouds and people. Easy to zoom out or close in to see the villagers at work. The missions give you something to achieve rather than just building aimlessly. Maybe just me, but I did find it difficult to get to the exact menu I wanted with the thumbstick, but you just want to keep playing even with this annoyance. Also find it hard to control my archers and soldiers. You can group them with a flag but they stray quite a bit to approach nearby enemies. It'd be nice if they would hold their positions. I needed to intervene a lot in the skirmishes. Also helping to move resources to the buildings, rather than waiting for the villagers to walk back and forth, really speeds things along.

reviews.review_title: Not normally my cup of tea but...
reviews.review_description: Im loving this experience. Everything so well. Im glad ive a battery pack as you loose the track of time and had to recharge after 4 hours. 10/10

reviews.review_title: How is this game not more famous?
reviews.review_description: There are so many unfinished and unpolished experiences in the Meta store. What a treat was to find out about this game. The graphics, level of detail, immersion, mechanics and the campaign are all excellent so far. It’s hard for me to feel this much engaged with a VR game. Totally recommend it.

This was so much fun. I finally beat it tonight after putting it down for awhile. I really enjoyed the missions and going from island to island. This was a gem! The controls were fun once you got used to them. I want more!!!

I had high hopes for this, but it was an outside game changed for Meta 3.  Some commands didn’t work, and I couldn’t play the game.  Even though I only had it a day or two, and played maybe one frustrating hour… it looks like I can’t get a refund.  😡
Great game fun for a quite a while. Please keep updating. Highly recommended

I just want to say what a fantastic game. I grew playing games on my pc in the 90s and early 2000s and this game takes me back so fantastically. I read the reviews and figured I’d give it a shot. People are trying to compare it to a game and most people say age of empires, but for those who can remember this gem, it reminds me so much of black and white. Genuinely my favorite game in VR. 

Devs, please make a sequel or at the least, create additional levels or make more content. I would even pay for additional content.

---

Townsmen VR is an absolutely fantastic game that immerses you in the charming world of city-building with a delightful twist. The combination of engaging gameplay, stunning visuals, and the joy of managing your medieval town brings back fond memories of games like Kingdom for Keflings, which I adored!

The level of interactivity in Townsmen VR is simply amazing. From interacting with your townsfolk to managing resources and expanding your city, every aspect feels rewarding and enjoyable. The VR experience takes it to another level, making you feel like you are truly part of the world you are creating. I believe the game deserves even more! There is so much potential for additional content and (DLC) that would enhance the gameplay experience and keep players coming back for more. Whether it’s new buildings, diverse themes, additional quests, or even new challenges to tackle, I’d gladly spend my money on expansions that enrich the game further.

Developers, if you're listening, please consider adding more updates and content to Townsmen VR. The community is eager for more adventures! Let’s continue this journey; I can already imagine how much richer the game could be!

In conclusion, Townsmen VR is a gem that deserves to shine even brighter with new updates and DLC. Let’s keep the excitement going and build a legacy together!

---

reviews.review_title: One of the few games I've completed!!!
reviews.review_description: What can I say about Townsmen? Oh boy. Just an amazing experience. It took me 20+ hours to finish the campaign (although I am very thorough and didn't just follow the missions linearly). I've seen comparisons to Deism but IT IS NOTHING LIKE DEISM, if you want to compare it to another game, Spacefolk City would be more apt. Anyway, a true VR experience unlike any other. You create towns from the ground up and manage your base, resources and armies. Some have mentioned that the game is repetitive but if you enjoy the challenge of having to adapt to new map structures and certain restrictions, this is for you. I thoroughly enjoyed the campaign and story and frickin love Sandbox mode. All my creativity and problem solving thinking is stretched in this game. Please give it a try. FOR DEVS: I hope you continue to add to the game or at least in this genre, this is just awesome and deserves DLCs, additional content and more mechanics. 10/10
It'd be so cool to have more scenarios to play!!!
Great game!
The select icon instruction is VERY VAGUE.  You have to point and click.  It makes you think you have to grab the icon.

reviews.review_title: I hope there are more games like this
reviews.review_description:
Love this game
So relaxing and fun
WAY TOO FUNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNN!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
